papers.abstract: LLM-powered multi-agent systems can now automate the full research pipeline from ideation to paper writing, but a fundamental question remains: automation for whom? Researchers operate under different resource configurations, hold different methodological preferences, and target different output formats. A system that produces uniform outputs regardless of these differences will systematically under-serve every individual user, making personalization a precondition for research automation to be genuinely usable. However, achieving it requires three capabilities that current systems lack: accumulating reusable procedural knowledge across projects, retaining user-specific experience across sessions, and internalizing implicit preferences that resist explicit formalization. We propose NanoResearch, a multi-agent framework that addresses these gaps through tri-level co-evolution. A skill bank distills recurring operations into compact procedural rules reusable across projects. A memory module maintains user- and project-specific experience that grounds planning decisions in each user's research history. A label-free policy learning converts free-form feedback into persistent parameter updates of the planner, reshaping subsequent coordination. These three layers co-evolve: reliable skills produce richer memory, richer memory informs better planning, and preference internalization continuously realigns the loop to each user. Extensive experiments demonstrate that NanoResearch delivers substantial gains over state-of-the-art AI research systems, and progressively refines itself to produce better research at lower cost over successive cycles.

analysis.tldr: 提出NanoResearch框架，通过技能库、记忆模块和无标签策略学习的三层协同进化，实现个性化研究自动化。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有LLM驱动的多智能体研究自动化系统忽视用户个性化需求，导致输出千篇一律，无法适应不同资源、偏好和输出格式，阻碍了实际可用性。
analysis.problem: 如何让研究自动化系统具备个性化能力，包括跨项目积累可复用知识、跨会话保留用户经验、内化难以形式化的隐式偏好。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出NanoResearch框架，包含三层协同进化：技能库（skill bank）将重复操作蒸馏为可复用规则；记忆模块（memory module）维护用户和项目特定经验；无标签策略学习（label-free policy learning）将自由反馈转化为规划器的持久参数更新。三层相互促进，实现持续个性化优化。
contributions: 提出NanoResearch框架，通过三层协同进化实现个性化研究自动化。
设计技能库、记忆模块和无标签策略学习机制，分别解决知识复用、经验保留和偏好内化问题。
实验证明NanoResearch优于现有AI研究系统，并能通过循环迭代自我优化。
analysis.experiments: 摘要提到大量实验表明NanoResearch相比最先进AI研究系统有显著提升，并能逐步自我优化以更低成本产出更好研究，但未提供具体数据集或指标。
limitations: 可能依赖用户反馈质量，自由反馈的噪声可能影响策略学习稳定性。
技能库的通用性与个性化之间的平衡可能需要进一步调优。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出NanoResearch框架，通过技能库、记忆模块和无标签策略学习的三层协同进化，实现个性化研究自动化。

papers.title: Reasoning Is Not Free: Robust Adaptive Cost-Efficient Routing for LLM-as-a-Judge
papers.abstract: Reasoning-capable large language models (LLMs) have recently been adopted as automated judges, but their benefits and costs in LLM-as-a-Judge settings remain unclear. Through controlled comparisons between reasoning and non-reasoning judges, we show that explicit reasoning substantially improves judgment accuracy on tasks requiring structured verification (e.g., math and coding), while offering limited or even negative gains on simpler evaluations and incurring significantly higher computational cost. These findings motivate that reasoning should be used selectively rather than universally, with awareness of possible distribution shift. We propose a Robust Adaptive Cost-Efficient Routing (RACER), which dynamically selects between reasoning and non-reasoning judges under a fixed budget by formulating routing as a constrained distributionally robust optimization problem. RACER explicitly accounts for distribution shift via a KL-divergence uncertainty set, admits an efficient primal--dual algorithm, and enjoys theoretical guarantees including uniqueness of the optimal policy and linear convergence. Extensive experiments show that RACER achieves superior accuracy--cost trade-offs under distribution shift.

analysis.tldr: 提出RACER框架，在LLM裁判任务中根据分布偏移动态选择推理/非推理模型，实现成本与准确率的帕累托最优。
analysis.research_motivation: 推理型LLM作为裁判时虽能提升结构化验证任务的准确率，但计算成本高昂，且对简单任务收益有限甚至为负，亟需一种成本感知的选择策略。
analysis.problem: 如何在固定预算下，针对不同评估任务动态选择推理型或非推理型LLM裁判，以在分布偏移场景下实现准确率与成本的最佳权衡。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 推理型裁判在数学、代码等结构化验证任务上准确率显著提升，但在简单评估任务上增益有限甚至为负，且计算成本远高于非推理模型。
analysis.method: 将路由问题建模为带KL散度不确定集的约束分布鲁棒优化，设计原始-对偶算法求解，保证策略唯一性和线性收敛。
contributions: 通过控制实验揭示推理型LLM裁判在不同任务上的收益-成本非单调关系
提出RACER框架，将路由形式化为分布鲁棒优化问题，显式建模分布偏移
给出理论保证（策略唯一性、线性收敛）并实验验证其准确率-成本权衡优势
analysis.experiments: 在多种LLM裁判任务上验证RACER在分布偏移下实现更优的准确率-成本权衡。
limitations: 仅考虑推理与非推理两类模型，未扩展至更多模型类型
KL散度不确定集可能无法覆盖所有实际分布偏移模式
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出RACER框架，在LLM裁判任务中根据分布偏移动态选择推理/非推理模型，实现成本与准确率的帕累托最优。

papers.title: New AI-Driven Tools for Enhancing Campus Well-being: A Prevention and Intervention Approach
papers.abstract: Campus well-being underpins academic success, yet many universities lack effective methods for monitoring satisfaction and detecting mental health risks. This dissertation addresses these gaps through prevention (improving feedback collection) and intervention (advancing mental health detection), unified under an integrated framework. For prevention, we developed TigerGPT, a personalized survey chatbot leveraging LLMs to engage users in context-aware conversations grounded in conversational design and engagement theory, achieving 75% usability and 81% satisfaction. To address its limitations in repetitiveness and response depth, we introduced AURA, a reinforcement-learning framework that adapts follow-up question types (validate, specify, reflect, probe) within a session using an LSDE quality signal (Length, Self-disclosure, Emotion, Specificity), initialized from 96 prior conversations. AURA achieved +0.12 mean quality gain (p=0.044, d=0.66), with 63% fewer specification prompts and 10x more validation behavior. For intervention, we examine Expressive Narrative Stories (ENS) for mental health screening, showing BERT(128) captures nuanced linguistic features without keyword cues, while conventional classifiers depend heavily on explicit mental health terms. We then developed PsychoGPT, an LLM built on DSM-5 and PHQ-8 guidelines that performs initial distress classification, symptom-level scoring, and reconciliation with external ratings for explainable assessment. To reduce hallucinations, we proposed Stacked Multi-Model Reasoning (SMMR), layering expert models where early layers handle localized subtasks and later layers reconcile findings, outperforming single-model solutions on DAIC-WOZ in accuracy, F1, and PHQ-8 scoring. Finally, a cohesive framework unifies these tools, enabling adaptive survey insights to flow directly into specialized mental health detection models.

analysis.tldr: 提出AI工具（TigerGPT、AURA、PsychoGPT）提升校园幸福感，涵盖预防性反馈收集与干预性心理健康检测。
analysis.research_motivation: 大学缺乏有效的满意度监测和心理健康风险检测方法，现有工具在用户参与度、检测准确性和可解释性方面存在不足。
analysis.problem: 如何通过AI驱动的预防（改进反馈收集）和干预（提升心理健康检测）来增强校园幸福感。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析，但指出TigerGPT存在重复性和响应深度不足的问题，以及传统分类器依赖显式心理健康术语。
analysis.method: 预防方面：TigerGPT（基于LLM的个性化调查聊天机器人）和AURA（强化学习框架，自适应调整追问类型）；干预方面：PsychoGPT（基于DSM-5和PHQ-8的LLM）和Stacked Multi-Model Reasoning（SMMR，分层专家模型减少幻觉）。
contributions: 提出TigerGPT和AURA用于预防性反馈收集，AURA通过强化学习提升对话质量。
开发PsychoGPT和SMMR用于可解释的心理健康检测，减少幻觉。
构建统一框架整合预防与干预工具。
analysis.experiments: TigerGPT达到75%可用性和81%满意度；AURA实现平均质量增益+0.12（p=0.044, d=0.66），规范提示减少63%，验证行为增加10倍；PsychoGPT在DAIC-WOZ上优于单模型方案。
limitations: TigerGPT存在重复性和响应深度不足。
PsychoGPT的幻觉问题虽通过SMMR缓解，但可能仍有残留。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出AI工具（TigerGPT、AURA、PsychoGPT）提升校园幸福感，涵盖预防性反馈收集与干预性心理健康检测。

papers.title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Football Performance Analysis: Evidence of Limited Transferability from Elite Leagues to University Competition
papers.abstract: Machine learning has become increasingly prevalent in football performance analysis, yet most studies prioritize predictive accuracy while implicitly assuming that learned performance determinants and their interpretations are transferable across competition levels. Whether interpretability remains reliable under domain shift-from elite to university football remains largely unexplored. This study investigates whether performance determinants learned from elite competitions are structurally transferable to university-level football and whether their interpretations remain robust under domain shift. Models were trained on large-scale event data from the top five European leagues and applied to university football data from National Tsing Hua University (NTHU) using an identical feature space. Random Forest and Multilayer Perceptron models were interpreted using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Counterfactual Impact Score (CIS). Across five experiments, elite football exhibited a stable and consistent hierarchy of performance determinants across leagues, models, and explanation methods. In contrast, NTHU university football showed substantial reordering of key indicators, reduced explanation stability, weaker structural agreement with elite domains, and increased sensitivity to explanation method. These findings suggest that interpretability robustness is domain-dependent. Rather than reflecting methodological limitations alone, instability in explanations under domain shift may serve as a diagnostic signal of structural ambiguity in the target domain.

analysis.tldr: 精英联赛学到的足球表现因素在大学比赛中不可迁移，解释稳定性随领域变化显著下降。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有机器学习足球分析大多假设跨竞赛级别的表现因素可迁移，但领域偏移下可解释性是否可靠尚未探索。
analysis.problem: 验证从精英联赛学到的表现决定因素及其解释是否在结构上可迁移至大学足球，并评估领域偏移下解释的鲁棒性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 精英联赛的表现因素层级稳定一致，而大学足球的关键指标排序显著变化、解释稳定性降低、与精英领域结构一致性弱，且对解释方法更敏感。
analysis.method: 使用随机森林和多层感知机在相同特征空间上训练，并用SHAP和反事实影响分数进行解释，通过五个实验比较跨领域解释稳定性。
contributions: 首次系统评估足球表现分析中跨竞赛级别的可解释性迁移性
发现解释不稳定性可作为目标领域结构模糊的诊断信号
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 仅涉及一个大学数据集，泛化性有限
未探讨其他解释方法或模型架构
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 精英联赛学到的足球表现因素在大学比赛中不可迁移，解释稳定性随领域变化显著下降。

papers.title: PathISE: Learning Informative Path Supervision for Knowledge Graph Question Answering
papers.abstract: Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA) aims to answer user questions by reasoning over Knowledge Graphs (KGs). Recent KGQA methods mainly follow the retrieval-augmented generation paradigm to ground Large Language Models~(LLMs) with structured knowledge from KGs. However, training effective models to retrieve question-relevant evidence from KGs typically requires high-quality intermediate supervision signals, such as question-relevant paths or subgraphs, which are time- and resource-intensive to obtain. We propose PathISE, a novel framework for learning high-quality intermediate supervision from answer-level labels. PathISE introduces a lightweight transformer-based estimator that estimates the informativeness of relation paths to construct pseudo path-level supervision. This supervision is then distilled into an LLM path generator, whose generated paths are grounded in the KG to provide compact evidence for inductive answer reasoning. ExtensiveISE experiments on three KGQA benchmarks show that PathISE achieves competitive or state-of-the-art KGQA performance, and provides reusable supervision signals that can enhance existing KGQA models, without relying on costly LLM-refined supervision signals. Our source code is available at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: PathISE通过从答案标签中学习路径级监督信号，无需昂贵的人工标注即可提升知识图谱问答性能。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有KGQA方法依赖高质量中间监督（如路径或子图）来训练检索模型，但这些监督信号获取成本高昂，限制了方法的可扩展性。
analysis.problem: 如何从答案标签中自动学习高质量的路径级监督信号，以训练LLM生成紧凑的证据路径用于KGQA。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: PathISE框架包含一个轻量级Transformer估计器，用于评估关系路径的信息量并构建伪路径监督，然后将该监督蒸馏到LLM路径生成器中，使其生成可被KG验证的路径。
contributions: 提出PathISE框架，从答案标签中学习路径级监督，无需昂贵的人工标注。
在三个KGQA基准上达到竞争或最优性能，且生成的监督信号可复用于增强现有KGQA模型。
analysis.experiments: 在三个KGQA基准上进行实验，结果表明PathISE取得了竞争性或最优性能，且无需LLM精炼的监督信号。
limitations: 可能依赖特定KG结构，泛化性需进一步验证。
伪监督质量可能受估计器精度影响。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: PathISE通过从答案标签中学习路径级监督信号，无需昂贵的人工标注即可提升知识图谱问答性能。

papers.title: ComplexMCP: Evaluation of LLM Agents in Dynamic, Interdependent, and Large-Scale Tool Sandbox
papers.abstract: Current LLM agents are proficient at calling isolated APIs but struggle with the "last mile" of commercial software automation. In real-world scenarios, tools are not independent; they are atomic, interdependent, and prone to environmental noise. We introduce $\textbf{ComplexMCP}$, a benchmark designed to evaluate agents in these rigorous conditions. Built on the Model Context Protocol (MCP), $\textbf{ComplexMCP}$ provides over 300 meticulously tested tools derived from 7 stateful sandboxes, ranging from office suites to financial systems. Unlike existing datasets, our benchmark utilizes a seed-driven architecture to simulate dynamic environment states and unpredictable API failures, ensuring a deterministic yet diverse evaluation. We evaluate various LLMs across full-context and RAG paradigms, revealing a stark performance gap: even top-tier models fail to exceed a 60% success rate, far trailing human performance 90%. Granular trajectory analysis identifies three fundamental bottlenecks: (1) $\textbf{tool retrieval saturation}$ as action spaces scale; (2) $\textbf{over-confidence}$, where agents skip essential environment verifications; and (3) $\textbf{strategic defeatism}$, a tendency to rationalize failure rather than pursuing recovery. These findings underscore the insufficiency of current agents for interdependent workflows, positioning $\textbf{ComplexMCP}$ as a critical testbed for the next generation of resilient autonomous systems.

analysis.tldr: ComplexMCP 是一个评估 LLM Agent 在动态、相互依赖、大规模工具沙箱中表现的基准，揭示了当前 Agent 在工具检索、过度自信和策略性放弃方面的瓶颈。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前 LLM Agent 擅长调用独立 API，但在商业软件自动化的“最后一英里”中表现不佳，因为现实场景中的工具是原子化、相互依赖且易受环境噪声影响的。
analysis.problem: 现有基准无法评估 Agent 在动态环境状态、工具相互依赖和不可预测 API 故障下的表现。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到顶级模型成功率不超过 60%，远低于人类 90% 的表现；轨迹分析识别出三个瓶颈：工具检索饱和、过度自信（跳过环境验证）和策略性放弃（将失败合理化而非恢复）。
analysis.method: 基于 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 构建包含 300+ 工具、7 个有状态沙箱的基准，采用种子驱动架构模拟动态环境状态和不可预测 API 故障。
contributions: 提出 ComplexMCP 基准，用于评估 Agent 在动态、相互依赖、大规模工具沙箱中的表现
通过实验揭示当前 Agent 在工具检索饱和、过度自信和策略性放弃方面的根本瓶颈
analysis.experiments: 评估了多种 LLM 在全上下文和 RAG 范式下的表现，发现顶级模型成功率不超过 60%，人类为 90%。
limitations: 基准仅覆盖 7 个领域，可能无法完全代表所有商业软件场景
未提供不同模型在瓶颈上的详细对比分析
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: ComplexMCP 是一个评估 LLM Agent 在动态、相互依赖、大规模工具沙箱中表现的基准，揭示了当前 Agent 在工具检索、过度自信和策略性放弃方面的瓶颈。

papers.title: TrajPrism: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Language-Grounded Urban Trajectory Understanding
papers.abstract: Urban mobility is naturally expressed both as trajectories in space and as natural-language descriptions of travel intent, constraints, and preferences. However, prior work rarely evaluates these two modalities together on the same real-world trajectories: trajectory modeling often stays geometry-centric, while language-centric mobility benchmarks frequently target route planning and tool use rather than fine-grained, verifiable alignment between text and the underlying route. We introduce TrajPrism, a multi-task benchmark for language-trajectory alignment that unifies (i) instruction-conditioned trajectory generation, (ii) language-driven semantic trajectory retrieval, and (iii) trajectory captioning, together with an evaluation protocol that measures trajectory fidelity, retrieval quality, and language groundedness. We construct TrajPrism by pairing real urban trajectories with judge-filtered language annotations generated under a four-dimensional travel-intent taxonomy. The benchmark contains 300K selected trajectories across Porto, San Francisco, and Beijing, yielding 2.1M task instances from three instruction variants, three retrieval queries, and one caption per trajectory. We further develop proof-of-concept models for each task: TrajAnchor for instruction-conditioned trajectory generation, TrajFuse for semantic trajectory retrieval, and TrajRap for trajectory captioning. These models instantiate the proposed tasks and show that geometry-only trajectory baselines leave a large gap on our protocol, especially where language is part of the input-output interface. We release TrajPrism with code and a reproducible annotation pipeline that is designed to be portable across cities, given compatible trajectory inputs and map resources.

analysis.tldr: 提出一个多任务基准TrajPrism，用于评估语言与城市轨迹的对齐能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有轨迹建模多关注几何特征，而语言导向的移动性基准则侧重于路线规划和工具使用，缺乏对文本与轨迹细粒度对齐的评估。
analysis.problem: 缺乏一个统一的多任务基准来评估语言与真实城市轨迹之间的对齐，包括轨迹生成、检索和描述。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 仅基于几何的轨迹基线在语言作为输入输出接口的任务上表现不佳，存在较大差距。
analysis.method: 构建TrajPrism基准，包含30万条轨迹和210万任务实例，涵盖三种指令变体、三种检索查询和每个轨迹一个描述；并开发了三个概念验证模型：TrajAnchor、TrajFuse和TrajRap。
contributions: 提出了一个多任务基准TrajPrism，用于语言-轨迹对齐，包含轨迹生成、检索和描述三个任务。
构建了大规模数据集，包含来自三个城市的30万条轨迹和210万任务实例，并提供了可复现的标注流程。
开发了三个概念验证模型，展示了仅几何基线的不足。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，但提到几何基线在语言接口任务上存在较大差距。
limitations: 可能仅适用于有兼容轨迹输入和地图资源的城市。
概念验证模型可能不是最优，存在改进空间。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一个多任务基准TrajPrism，用于评估语言与城市轨迹的对齐能力。

papers.title: MATRA: Modeling the Attack Surface of Agentic AI Systems -- OpenClaw Case Study
papers.abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents with access to tools, databases, and external services, yet practitioners (across different sectors) lack systematic methods to assess how known threat classes translate into concrete risks within a specific agentic deployment. We present MATRA, a pragmatic threat modeling framework for agentic AI systems that adapts established risk assessment methodology to systematically assess how known LLM threats translate into deployment-specific risks. MATRA begins with an asset-based impact assessment and utilizes attack trees to determine the likelihood of these impacts occurring within the system architecture. We demonstrate MATRA on a personal AI agent deployment using OpenClaw, quantifying how architectural controls such as network sandboxing and least-privilege access reduce risk by limiting the blast radius of successful injections.

analysis.tldr: 提出MATRA框架，系统评估AI代理系统的攻击面，并通过OpenClaw案例量化架构控制对风险的降低效果。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM作为自主代理部署时面临已知威胁，但缺乏系统方法将这些威胁转化为具体部署中的风险评估。
analysis.problem: 如何系统评估AI代理系统在特定部署中的攻击面，并量化架构控制对风险的影响。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: MATRA框架：基于资产的冲击评估结合攻击树分析，量化架构控制（如网络沙箱、最小权限）对风险降低的效果。
contributions: 提出MATRA威胁建模框架，适配已知LLM威胁到具体部署风险。
通过OpenClaw案例演示框架应用，量化架构控制对风险降低的影响。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 框架依赖专家知识构建攻击树，可能难以自动化。
案例仅针对个人AI代理，泛化性需进一步验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出MATRA框架，系统评估AI代理系统的攻击面，并通过OpenClaw案例量化架构控制对风险的降低效果。

papers.title: The Agent Use of Agent Beings: Agent Cybernetics Is the Missing Science of Foundation Agents
papers.abstract: LLM-based foundation agents that perceive, reason, and act across thousands of reasoning steps are rapidly becoming the dominant paradigm for deploying artificial intelligence in open-ended, long-horizon complex tasks. Despite this significance, the field remains overwhelmingly engineering-driven. Engineering practice has converged on useful primitives (tool loops, memory banks, harnesses, reflection steps), yet these are assembled by empirical trial and error rather than from first principles. Fundamental questions remain open: under what conditions does a long-running agent remain on-task? How should an agent respond when its environment exceeds its representational capacity? What architectural properties are necessary for safe self-improvement? We argue that cybernetics, the mid-twentieth-century science of control and communication in complex systems, provides the missing theoretical scaffold for foundation agents. By mapping six canonical laws of classical cybernetics onto six agent design principles, and synthesizing those principles into three engineering desiderata (reliability, lifelong running, and self-Improvement), we arrive at a framework termed Agent Cybernetics. Three application domains, code generation, computer use and automated research, exemplify the analytical framework of agent cybernetics by identifying failure modes and concrete engineering recommendations. We hope that agent cybernetics opens a new research venue and establishes the scientific foundation that foundation agents need for principled, reliable real-world deployment.

analysis.tldr: 提出Agent控制论作为基础Agent的理论框架，将经典控制论定律映射为Agent设计原则。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前基于LLM的基础Agent开发主要依赖工程试错，缺乏理论指导，导致在可靠性、长期运行和自我改进等关键问题上缺乏原则性理解。
analysis.problem: 缺乏一个系统的理论框架来指导基础Agent的设计，使其能够可靠地部署在开放、长期、复杂的任务中。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出工程实践已收敛于一些有用原语（如工具循环、记忆库等），但缺乏第一性原理指导，且存在未解决的基本问题（如长期运行保持任务、环境超出表征能力时的响应、安全自我改进所需架构属性）。
analysis.method: 将经典控制论的六条规范定律映射为六条Agent设计原则，并综合为三个工程需求（可靠性、终身运行、自我改进），形成Agent控制论框架。
contributions: 提出Agent控制论作为基础Agent的科学理论框架
将六条控制论定律映射为Agent设计原则，并导出三个工程需求
在代码生成、计算机使用和自动化研究三个领域展示框架的分析能力
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，仅提及在三个应用领域通过识别失败模式和工程建议来例证框架。
limitations: 框架目前是理论性的，缺乏大规模实证验证
映射关系可能不够精确，需要进一步细化
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出Agent控制论作为基础Agent的理论框架，将经典控制论定律映射为Agent设计原则。

papers.title: GESR: A Genetic Programming-Based Symbolic Regression Method with Gene Editing
papers.abstract: Mathematical formulas serve as a language through which humans communicate with nature. Discovering mathematical laws from scientific data to describe natural phenomena has been a long-standing pursuit of humanity for centuries. In the field of artificial intelligence, this challenge is known as the symbolic regression problem. Among existing symbolic regression approaches, Genetic Programming (GP) based on evolutionary algorithms remains one of the most classical and widely adopted methods. GP simulates the evolutionary process across generations through genetic mutation and crossover. However, mutations and crossovers in GP are entirely random. While this randomness effectively mimics natural evolution, it inevitably produces both beneficial and detrimental variations. If there existed a metaphorical `God` capable of foreseeing which genetic mutations or crossovers would yield superior outcomes and performing targeted gene editing accordingly, the efficiency of evolution could be substantially improved. Motivated by this idea, we propose in this paper a symbolic regression approach based on gene editing, termed GESR. In GESR, we trained two "hands of God" (two BERT models). Among them, the first leverages the BERT's masked language modeling capability to guide the mutation of genes (expression symbols). The other BERT model guides the crossover of individual genes by predicting the crossover point. Experimental results demonstrate that GESR significantly improves computational efficiency compared with traditional GP algorithms and achieves strong overall performance across multiple symbolic regression tasks.

analysis.tldr: GESR 利用两个 BERT 模型作为“上帝之手”指导遗传编程中的基因编辑，显著提升符号回归效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 遗传编程（GP）在符号回归中广泛使用，但其随机变异和交叉会产生大量无效变异，导致效率低下。
analysis.problem: 如何通过有指导的基因编辑替代随机变异和交叉，提高遗传编程在符号回归中的效率。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 训练两个 BERT 模型：一个利用掩码语言建模指导基因（表达式符号）的变异，另一个预测交叉点指导基因交叉。
contributions: 提出基于基因编辑的符号回归方法 GESR，用 BERT 模型指导遗传编程的变异和交叉。
实验表明 GESR 相比传统 GP 显著提升计算效率，并在多个符号回归任务上取得强整体性能。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能依赖 BERT 模型的训练质量，泛化性需进一步验证。
未提及与最新深度符号回归方法的对比。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: GESR 利用两个 BERT 模型作为“上帝之手”指导遗传编程中的基因编辑，显著提升符号回归效率。

papers.title: Evolving-RL: End-to-End Optimization of Experience-Driven Self-Evolving Capability within Agents
papers.abstract: Experience-driven self-evolving agents aim to overcome the static nature of large language models by distilling reusable experience from past interactions, thus enabling adaptation to novel tasks at deployment time. This process places substantial demands on the foundation model's capacities for abstraction, generalization, and in-context learning. However, most existing studies focus primarily on system-level design choices, such as how experience is represented and managed, neglecting the inherent capabilities of the underlying model. While some recent works have started to optimize the experience utilization stage via reinforcement learning, they still fail to treat self-evolution as a unified process to be jointly optimized. To this end, we propose Evolving-RL, an efficient algorithmic framework that jointly improves the experience extraction and utilization capabilities required for self-evolution. Specifically, we center the learning process on experience extraction and evaluation, using the two supervisory signals derived from evaluation to optimize the extractor and solver separately and thus enable their coordinated co-evolution. Experiments on ALFWorld and Mind2Web show that Evolving-RL effectively enhances LLMs' ability to extract and reuse experience, leading to strong performance gains on out-of-distribution tasks (up to 98.7% relative improvement over the GRPO baseline on ALFWorld unseen tasks and 35.8% on Mind2Web), and these gains are fully unlocked only through the coordinated co-evolution of experience extraction and utilization. Furthermore, Evolving-RL inherently functions as an experience-augmented RL algorithm. By internalizing reusable experience patterns directly into model parameters, it achieves remarkable performance gains over standard baselines on both seen and unseen tasks, even in the absence of test-time experience accumulation.

analysis.tldr: 提出Evolving-RL框架，通过强化学习联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，实现端到端的自我进化。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有经验驱动型自进化智能体主要关注系统级设计（如经验表示与管理），忽视了底层模型的内在能力，且未将自进化视为统一过程进行联合优化。
analysis.problem: 如何端到端地联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，以提升其在部署时对新颖任务的适应性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体的现象分析，但指出仅优化经验利用阶段（如通过强化学习）而不联合优化提取阶段，无法充分释放自进化潜力。
analysis.method: 提出Evolving-RL框架，以经验提取和评估为中心，利用评估产生的两个监督信号分别优化提取器（extractor）和求解器（solver），实现两者的协同共进化。
contributions: 提出Evolving-RL，首个端到端联合优化经验提取与利用的算法框架。
在ALFWorld和Mind2Web上验证了协同共进化对提升分布外任务性能的关键作用，最高相对提升98.7%。
揭示Evolving-RL本质上是经验增强的强化学习算法，无需测试时经验积累即可内化经验模式。
analysis.experiments: 在ALFWorld和Mind2Web上评估，与GRPO基线相比，在未见任务上分别获得最高98.7%和35.8%的相对提升。
limitations: 可能依赖任务环境提供的经验评估信号，通用性待验证。
框架在更复杂或开放域任务上的扩展性未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出Evolving-RL框架，通过强化学习联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，实现端到端的自我进化。

papers.title: diffGHOST: Diffusion based Generative Hedged Oblivious Synthetic Trajectories
papers.abstract: Trajectories are nowadays valuable information for a wide range of applications. However they are also inherently sensitive, as they contain highly personal information about individuals. Facing this challenge, synthesizing mobility trajectories has emerged as a promising solution to leverage mobility information while preserving privacy. State-of-the-art models, often rely on the false assumptions of generative models implicit privacy and fails to provide privacy guarantees while preserving trajectories utility. Here, we introduce diffGHOST, a conditional diffusion model based on latent space segmentation, designed to answer this challenge. Thus, this paper propose a methodology that identify and mitigate memorization of critical samples using condition segments of a learn latent space.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于条件扩散模型的轨迹生成方法，通过潜在空间分割缓解记忆问题，实现隐私保护与效用平衡。
analysis.research_motivation: 轨迹数据包含高度个人信息，现有生成模型缺乏隐私保证，难以在保护隐私的同时保持轨迹效用。
analysis.problem: 如何生成具有隐私保证且保持高可用性的合成轨迹数据。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 现有生成模型隐式假设隐私保护，但实际存在记忆关键样本的风险，导致隐私泄露。
analysis.method: 基于潜在空间分割的条件扩散模型，通过识别和缓解关键样本的记忆来生成合成轨迹。
contributions: 提出diffGHOST，一种基于潜在空间分割的条件扩散模型用于轨迹生成。
提出识别和缓解关键样本记忆的方法，提供隐私保证。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能仅在特定轨迹数据集上验证，泛化性未知。
隐私与效用的权衡可能未达到最优。
analysis.primary_area_en: generative models
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于条件扩散模型的轨迹生成方法，通过潜在空间分割缓解记忆问题，实现隐私保护与效用平衡。

papers.title: Navigating the Sea of LLM Evaluation: Investigating Bias in Toxicity Benchmarks
papers.abstract: The rapid adoption of LLMs in both research and industry highlights the challenges of deploying them safely and reveals a gap in the systematic evaluation of toxicity benchmarks. As organizations increasingly rely on these benchmarks to certify models for customer-facing applications and automated moderation, unrecognized evaluation biases could lead to the deployment of vulnerable or unsafe systems. This work investigates the robustness of established benchmarking setups and examines how to measure currently neglected intrinsic biases, such as those related to model choice, metrics, and task types. Our experiments uncover significant discrepancies in benchmark behaviors when evaluation setups are altered. Specifically, shifting the task from text completion to summarization increases the tendency of benchmarks to flag content as harmful. Additionally, certain benchmarks fail to maintain consistent behavior when the input data domain is changed. Furthermore, we observe model-specific instabilities, demonstrating a clear need for more robust and comprehensive safety evaluation frameworks.

analysis.tldr: 研究发现毒性基准在评估设置变化时存在显著偏差，需更稳健的安全评估框架。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM安全部署依赖毒性基准，但现有基准可能存在未被识别的评估偏差，导致不安全系统被部署。
analysis.problem: 毒性基准在模型选择、指标、任务类型等设置变化时表现不一致，缺乏鲁棒性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 任务从文本补全改为摘要时，基准更易标记内容为有害；输入数据域变化时某些基准行为不一致；模型特定不稳定性存在。
analysis.method: 通过改变评估设置（任务类型、数据域、模型）系统性测量基准偏差。
contributions: 揭示毒性基准在评估设置变化下的显著偏差
提出需要更稳健和全面的安全评估框架
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 未提出具体缓解偏差的方法
可能未覆盖所有类型的偏差
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 研究发现毒性基准在评估设置变化时存在显著偏差，需更稳健的安全评估框架。

papers.title: Teacher-Aware Evolution of Heuristic Programs from Learned Optimization Policies
papers.abstract: LLM-based automatic heuristic design has shown promise for generating executable heuristics for combinatorial optimization, but existing methods mainly rely on delayed endpoint performance. We propose a \emph{teacher-aware evolutionary framework} that uses independently trained learned optimization policies as behavioral teachers. Instead of deploying or imitating the teacher, our method queries it on states visited by candidate heuristic programs and uses its action preferences as local feedback for evolution. The resulting search discovers static executable heuristics guided by both task performance and teacher-derived behavioral signals. Experiments on scheduling, routing, and graph optimization benchmarks show that our method improves over performance-driven LLM heuristic evolution baselines while requiring no neural inference at deployment. These results suggest that learned optimization policies can be repurposed as behavioral feedback sources for automatic heuristic discovery.

analysis.tldr: 利用学习到的优化策略作为行为教师，引导进化搜索生成静态可执行启发式程序。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有基于LLM的自动启发式设计主要依赖最终性能反馈，搜索效率低且缺乏中间指导。
analysis.problem: 如何利用学习到的优化策略作为行为教师，为启发式程序的进化提供局部反馈，从而生成更优的静态可执行启发式。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出教师感知进化框架，使用独立训练的学习优化策略作为行为教师，在候选启发式程序访问的状态上查询教师动作偏好作为局部反馈，结合任务性能进行进化搜索。
contributions: 提出教师感知进化框架，利用学习到的优化策略作为行为教师提供局部反馈。
在调度、路由和图优化基准上验证了方法优于仅依赖性能的LLM启发式进化基线。
analysis.experiments: 在调度、路由和图优化基准上进行实验，结果表明方法优于性能驱动的LLM启发式进化基线，且部署时无需神经推理。
limitations: 依赖预训练的学习优化策略的质量和可用性。
可能仅适用于可定义状态空间的组合优化问题。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 利用学习到的优化策略作为行为教师，引导进化搜索生成静态可执行启发式程序。

papers.title: Hierarchical Causal Abduction: A Foundation Framework for Explainable Model Predictive Control
papers.abstract: Model Predictive Control (MPC) is widely used to operate safety-critical infrastructure by predicting future trajectories and optimizing control actions. However, nonlinear dynamics, hard safety constraints, and numerical optimization often render individual control moves opaque to human operators, undermining trust and hindering deployment. This paper presents Hierarchical Causal Abduction (HCA), which combines (i) physics-informed reasoning via domain knowledge graphs, (ii) optimization evidence from Karush--Kuhn--Tucker (KKT) multipliers, and (iii) temporal causal discovery via the PCMCI algorithm to generate faithful, human-interpretable explanations for control actions computed by nonlinear MPC. Across three diverse control applications (greenhouse climate, building HVAC, chemical process engineering) with expert validation, HCA improves explanation accuracy by 53\% over LIME (0.478 vs. 0.311) using a single set of cross-domain parameters without per-domain tuning; domain-specific KKT-threshold calibration over 2--3 days further increases accuracy to 0.88. Ablation studies confirm that each evidence source is essential, with 32--37\% accuracy degradation when any component is removed, and HCA's ranking-and-validation methodology generalizes beyond MPC to other prediction-based decision systems, including learning-based control and trajectory planning.

analysis.tldr: 提出分层因果溯因框架，结合物理知识、KKT乘子和因果发现为非线性MPC生成可解释控制决策，准确率显著优于LIME。
analysis.research_motivation: 非线性MPC因动力学复杂、约束严格和数值优化导致控制动作不透明，削弱操作者信任，阻碍部署。
analysis.problem: 为非线性MPC计算的控制动作生成忠实、人类可理解的解释。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 分层因果溯因（HCA），融合物理信息推理（领域知识图谱）、优化证据（KKT乘子）和时间因果发现（PCMCI算法）。
contributions: 提出HCA框架，整合三种证据源为非线性MPC生成可解释性解释。
在三个不同控制领域（温室气候、建筑HVAC、化工过程）验证，跨域参数下解释准确率比LIME提升53%。
消融实验表明每个证据源不可或缺，移除任一组件准确率下降32-37%。
analysis.experiments: 在温室气候、建筑HVAC、化工过程三个控制应用上，经专家验证，HCA跨域参数下准确率0.478 vs LIME 0.311；领域特定KKT阈值校准2-3天后准确率达0.88。
limitations: 依赖领域知识图谱的构建质量。
KKT阈值校准需要2-3天领域特定调参。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出分层因果溯因框架，结合物理知识、KKT乘子和因果发现为非线性MPC生成可解释控制决策，准确率显著优于LIME。

papers.title: PRISM: Generation-Time Detection and Mitigation of Secret Leakage in Multi-Agent LLM Pipelines
papers.abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems introduce a security risk in which sensitive information accessed by one agent can propagate through shared context and reappear in downstream outputs, even without explicit adversarial intent. We formalise this phenomenon as propagation amplification, where leakage risk increases across agent boundaries as sensitive content is repeatedly exposed to downstream generators. Existing defences, including prompt-based safeguards, static pattern matching, and LLM-as-judge filtering, are not designed for this setting: they either operate after generation, rely primarily on surface-form patterns, or add substantial latency without modelling the generation process itself. To resolve these issues, we propose PRISM, a real-time defence that treats credential leakage as a sequential risk accumulation problem during generation. At each decoding step, PRISM combines 16 signals spanning lexical, structural, information-theoretic, behavioural, and contextual features into a calibrated risk score, enabling per-token intervention through green, yellow, and red risk zones. Our central observation is that credential reproduction is often preceded by a measurable shift in generation dynamics, characterised by entropy collapse and increasing logit concentration. When combined with text-structural cues such as identifier-pattern detection, these temporal signals provide an early warning of leakage before a secret is fully reconstructed. Across a 2,000-task adversarial benchmark covering 13 attack categories and three pressure levels in a heterogeneous four-agent pipeline, PRISM achieves F1 = 0.832 with precision = 1.000 and recall = 0.712, while producing no observed leakage on our benchmark (0.0% task-level leak rate) and preserving output utility of 0.893. It substantially outperforms the strongest baseline, Span Tagger, which achieves F1 = 0.719 with a 15.0% task-level leak rate.

analysis.tldr: PRISM 在生成过程中实时检测并阻止多智能体 LLM 流水线中的秘密泄露，通过逐 token 风险评分实现零泄露。
analysis.research_motivation: 多智能体 LLM 系统中，一个智能体访问的敏感信息可能通过共享上下文传播到下游输出，造成安全风险。现有防御方法（如提示词防护、静态模式匹配、LLM 作为裁判过滤）不适用于这种场景，它们要么在生成后操作，要么依赖表面形式，要么增加延迟而不建模生成过程。
analysis.problem: 多智能体 LLM 流水线中敏感信息（如凭证）的传播放大问题，即泄露风险随智能体边界增加而累积，需要一种实时、低延迟的防御方法。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 凭证再现前通常会出现生成动态的可测量变化，表现为熵崩溃和 logit 集中度增加，结合文本结构线索（如标识符模式检测）可提供早期预警。
analysis.method: PRISM 将凭证泄露视为生成过程中的顺序风险累积问题，在每个解码步骤结合 16 个信号（涵盖词汇、结构、信息论、行为、上下文特征）计算校准风险分数，并通过绿、黄、红三个风险区域实现逐 token 干预。
contributions: 形式化定义了多智能体 LLM 流水线中的传播放大现象。
提出 PRISM，一种实时、逐 token 的泄露检测与缓解方法，利用生成动态变化作为早期预警信号。
构建了一个包含 13 种攻击类别和三种压力水平的 2000 任务对抗基准，用于评估多智能体流水线中的泄露。
analysis.experiments: 在异构四智能体流水线上，针对 2000 任务对抗基准（13 种攻击类别、三种压力水平），PRISM 达到 F1=0.832（精确率=1.000，召回率=0.712），任务级泄露率为 0.0%，输出效用保持 0.893，显著优于最强基线 Span Tagger（F1=0.719，泄露率 15.0%）。
limitations: 可能仅适用于文本生成场景，对其他模态（如图像、音频）的泄露未验证。
16 个信号的计算可能增加推理开销，尽管摘要未提及具体延迟。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: PRISM 在生成过程中实时检测并阻止多智能体 LLM 流水线中的秘密泄露，通过逐 token 风险评分实现零泄露。

papers.title: The Open-Box Fallacy: Why AI Deployment Needs a Calibrated Verification Regime
papers.abstract: AI deployment in sensitive domains such as health care, credit, employment, and criminal justice is often treated as unsafe to authorize until model internals can be explained. This often leads to an excessive reliance on mechanistic interpretability to address a deployment challenge beyond its intended scope. We argue that the gate should instead be calibrated verification: authorization should be domain-scoped, independently checkable, monitored after release, accountable, contestable, and revocable. The reason is twofold. First, model capability is uneven across nearby tasks, so authorization must attach to a specific use rather than to a model in general. Second, societies have long governed opaque expertise through credentials, monitoring, liability, appeal, and revocation rather than mechanism-level explanation. Recent evidence reinforces this distinction between mechanistic understanding and deployment authority: a 53-percentage-point gap between internal representations and output correction shows that understanding may not translate into action, while one scoping review found that only 9.0% of FDA-approved AI/ML device documents contained a prospective post-market surveillance study. We propose Verification Coverage, a six-component reportable standard with a minimum-composition rule, as the metric that should sit beside capability scores in model cards, leaderboards, and regulatory disclosures.

analysis.tldr: 论文提出AI部署应转向“校准验证”而非依赖机制可解释性，并引入“验证覆盖度”标准。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前AI在医疗、信贷等敏感领域部署时，过度依赖机制可解释性作为授权前提，但可解释性无法保证实际部署安全，且存在理解与行动脱节的问题。
analysis.problem: 解决AI部署授权标准不当的问题，即如何从依赖模型内部解释转向基于领域范围、可独立检查、可监控、可问责、可争议和可撤销的校准验证。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到两个现象：1) 模型能力在邻近任务间不均匀，授权应针对具体用途而非通用模型；2) 社会长期通过凭证、监控、责任、申诉和撤销来管理不透明专业知识，而非机制级解释。实证显示内部表征与输出修正之间存在53个百分点的差距，且仅9.0%的FDA批准的AI/ML设备文档包含前瞻性上市后监测研究。
analysis.method: 提出“验证覆盖度”（Verification Coverage），一个六组件可报告标准，包含最小组成规则，作为模型卡、排行榜和监管披露中与能力分数并列的指标。
contributions: 指出机制可解释性在部署授权中的局限性，提出校准验证作为替代框架。
提出验证覆盖度标准，为AI部署提供可操作、可报告的度量。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 验证覆盖度的具体实施细节和有效性尚未在摘要中验证。
可能面临监管采纳和行业标准化的挑战。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 论文提出AI部署应转向“校准验证”而非依赖机制可解释性，并引入“验证覆盖度”标准。

papers.title: Budget-Efficient Automatic Algorithm Design via Code Graph
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have emerged as powerful tools for automatic algorithm design (AAD). However, existing pipelines remain inefficient. They operate at the granularity of full algorithms, redundantly rewriting recurring substructures and discarding low-fitness candidates that may contain valuable algorithmic features. We formalize budget-efficient automatic algorithm design, wherein the search policy maximizes realized fitness subject to limited computational cost. We propose a directed acyclic graph representation of algorithms and build a search framework that fully exploits the LLM's output. Instead of querying the LLM for full algorithms, we use it to obtain corrections: compact operators that add, replace, or remove code blocks. Each correction augments the graph, yielding new algorithms that compose with prior corrections. This graph structure decomposes algorithms into sets of corrections, enabling correction-level credit assignment that informs subsequent queries. We complement this framework with theoretical insights into the ideal balance between search depth and breadth at different budget levels. We validate our method empirically on three combinatorial optimization problems, demonstrating consistent superiority of our graph-based search over full-algorithm search at equal token budget. Finally, our experiments suggest that rich contexts help only when the LLM's prior knowledge is shallow, and can hinder performance otherwise.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于代码图的预算高效自动算法设计框架，通过增量修正替代完整算法生成，显著提升搜索效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有LLM驱动的自动算法设计方法效率低下，以完整算法为粒度重复生成冗余子结构，且丢弃低适应度候选中的有价值特征，导致计算成本高。
analysis.problem: 如何在有限计算预算下最大化自动算法设计的搜索效率，避免冗余生成并利用部分有效信息。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 将算法表示为有向无环图，使用LLM生成增量修正（添加、替换、删除代码块），每个修正扩展图结构，实现修正级别的信用分配，并理论分析搜索深度与广度的最优平衡。
contributions: 形式化预算高效自动算法设计问题，提出基于代码图的搜索框架。
理论推导不同预算下搜索深度与广度的最优平衡。
在三个组合优化问题上验证图搜索优于完整算法搜索。
analysis.experiments: 在三个组合优化问题上进行实验，比较图搜索与完整算法搜索在相同token预算下的性能，结果显示图搜索一致更优。
limitations: 仅验证了组合优化问题，未涉及其他领域。
依赖LLM生成修正的质量，可能受限于模型能力。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于代码图的预算高效自动算法设计框架，通过增量修正替代完整算法生成，显著提升搜索效率。

papers.title: LLARS: Enabling Domain Expert & Developer Collaboration for LLM Prompting, Generation and Evaluation
papers.abstract: We demonstrate LLARS (LLM Assisted Research System), an open-source platform that bridges the gap between domain experts and developers for building LLM-based systems. It integrates three tightly connected modules into an end-to-end pipeline: Collaborative Prompt Engineering for real-time co-authoring with version control and instant LLM testing, Batch Generation for configurable output production across user-selected prompts $\times$ models $\times$ data with cost control, and Hybrid Evaluation where human and LLM evaluators jointly assess outputs through diverse assessment methods, with live agreement metrics and provenance analysis to identify the best model-prompt combination for a given use case. New prompts and models are automatically available for batch generation and completed batches can be turned into evaluation scenarios with a single click. Interviews with six domain experts and three developers in online counselling confirmed that LLARS feels intuitive, saves considerable time by keeping everything in one place and makes interdisciplinary collaboration seamless.

analysis.tldr: LLARS是一个开源平台，通过协作提示工程、批量生成和混合评估模块，促进领域专家与开发者共同构建LLM系统。
analysis.research_motivation: 构建LLM系统时，领域专家与开发者之间存在协作鸿沟，现有工具缺乏端到端集成，导致效率低下。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一个平台，让非技术领域专家和开发者能高效协作完成LLM提示工程、生成和评估。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出LLARS平台，包含三个模块：协作提示工程（实时共同编辑、版本控制、即时测试）、批量生成（可配置输出、成本控制）、混合评估（人类与LLM联合评估、一致性指标和溯源分析）。
contributions: 提出一个集成提示工程、批量生成和混合评估的端到端开源平台。
通过在线咨询领域的用户研究验证了平台的易用性和协作效率。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，仅提及对六位领域专家和三位开发者的访谈确认了平台直观、省时且促进协作。
limitations: 用户研究样本量较小，可能不具广泛代表性。
平台功能可能依赖特定LLM后端，通用性待验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLARS是一个开源平台，通过协作提示工程、批量生成和混合评估模块，促进领域专家与开发者共同构建LLM系统。

papers.title: A Resilient Solution for Sewer Overflow Monitoring across Cloud and Edge
papers.abstract: Aging combined sewer systems in many historical cities are increasingly stressed by extreme rainfall events, which can trigger combined sewer overflows (CSO) with significant environmental and public health impacts. Forecasting the filling dynamics of overflow basins is critical for anticipating capacity exceedance and enabling timely preventive actions for CSO. We present a web-based demonstrator ( this https URL ) that integrates Deep Learning forecasting methods in both cloud and edge settings into an interactive monitoring dashboard for overflow monitoring, resilient to network outages. A video showcase is available online ( this https URL ).

analysis.tldr: 提出一个结合深度学习的云边协同下水道溢流监测系统，提升网络中断下的韧性。
analysis.research_motivation: 历史城市的老化合流制下水道系统在极端降雨下易发生溢流，造成环境和健康危害，现有监测方案在断网时失效。
analysis.problem: 如何构建一个在云和边缘端均能运行、且对网络中断具有韧性的下水道溢流预测与监测系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 开发一个基于Web的演示系统，将深度学习预测方法部署在云端和边缘端，集成到交互式监测仪表板中，实现网络中断下的韧性运行。
contributions: 提出一个云边协同的CSO监测架构，支持网络中断时边缘端独立运行。
开发了在线演示系统和视频展示，验证了系统可行性。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 未提供定量性能评估。
可能仅针对特定流域或数据集。

papers.title: LLM Jaggedness Unlocks Scientific Creativity
papers.abstract: As artificial intelligence advances, models are not improving uniformly. Instead, progress unfolds in a jagged fashion, with capabilities growing unevenly across tasks, domains, and model scales. In this work, we examine this dynamic jaggedness through the lens of scientific idea generation. We introduce SciAidanBench, a benchmark of open-ended scientific questions designed to measure the scientific creativity of large language models (LLMs). Given a scientific question, models are asked to generate as many unique and coherent ideas as possible, with the total number of valid responses serving as a proxy for creative potential. Evaluating 19 base models across 8 providers (30 total variants including reasoning versions), we find that jaggedness manifests both across models and within models. First, in a cross-task comparison between general and scientific creativity, improvements in general creativity do not translate uniformly to scientific creativity, revealing divergent capability profiles across models. Second, at the prompt level, stronger models do not improve uniformly; instead, they exhibit high variability, with bursts of creativity on some questions and limited performance on others. Third, at the domain level, individual models display uneven strengths across scientific subfields, reflecting fragmented internal capability profiles. Finally, we show that this jaggedness can be harnessed. We explore mechanisms of inference-time compute, knowledge pooling, and brainstorming to combine models effectively and construct meta-model ensembles that outperform any single model. Our results position jaggedness not as a limitation, but as a resource, a structural feature of AI progress that, when understood and leveraged, can amplify LLM-driven scientific creativity.

analysis.tldr: LLM能力的不均匀增长（锯齿性）可被利用来提升科学创造力，通过集成多个模型超越单一模型。
analysis.research_motivation: AI模型能力并非均匀提升，而是呈锯齿状分布，这种不均匀性在科学创意生成中可能被忽视或视为缺陷，但实际可被利用来增强创造力。
analysis.problem: 如何量化和利用LLM在科学创意生成中的能力不均匀性（锯齿性）以提升整体创造力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到：1) 通用创造力与科学创造力提升不一致；2) 同一模型在不同问题上的创造力波动大；3) 模型在不同科学子领域表现不均。
analysis.method: 提出SciAidanBench基准，评估19个基础模型（30个变体）的科学创意生成能力，并通过推理时计算、知识池化和头脑风暴等机制构建元模型集成。
contributions: 引入SciAidanBench基准，用于评估LLM在开放式科学问题上的创意生成能力。
系统揭示LLM在科学创意生成中的锯齿性现象（跨任务、跨问题、跨领域）。
证明通过模型集成可有效利用锯齿性，提升科学创造力。
analysis.experiments: 在19个基础模型（30个变体）上评估，使用SciAidanBench，通过生成独特且连贯的想法数量衡量创造力，并比较集成模型与单一模型性能。
limitations: 基准仅覆盖科学问题，可能不适用于其他创意领域。
集成方法可能增加计算成本。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLM能力的不均匀增长（锯齿性）可被利用来提升科学创造力，通过集成多个模型超越单一模型。

papers.title: Deep Arguing
papers.abstract: Deep learning has become the dominant approach for creating high capacity, scalable models across diverse data modalities. However, because these models rely on a large number of learned parameters, tightly couple feature extraction with task objectives, and often lack explicit reasoning mechanisms, it is difficult for humans to understand how they arrive at their predictions. Understanding what representations emerge and why they arise from the training data remains an open challenge. We introduce Deep Arguing, a novel neurosymbolic approach that integrates deep learning with argumentation construction and reasoning for interpretable classification with different data modalities. In our approach deep neural networks construct an argumentation structure wherein data points support their assigned label and attack different ones. Using differentiable argumentation semantics for reasoning, the model is trained end-to-end to jointly learn feature representation and argumentative interactions. This results in argumentation structures providing faithful case-based explanations for predictions. Structure constraints over the argumentation graph guide learning, improving both interpretability and predictive performance. Experiments with tabular and imaging datasets show that Deep Arguing achieves performance competitive with standard baselines whilst offering interpretable argumentative reasoning.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种神经符号方法Deep Arguing，将深度学习与论证推理结合，实现可解释分类。
analysis.research_motivation: 深度学习模型依赖大量参数、紧密耦合特征提取与任务目标、缺乏显式推理机制，导致人类难以理解其预测过程，亟需可解释方法。
analysis.problem: 如何让深度学习模型在保持性能的同时提供忠实、可理解的基于案例的解释。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: Deep Arguing：深度神经网络构建论证结构，数据点支持其标签并攻击其他标签；通过可微论证语义进行端到端训练，联合学习特征表示和论证交互。
contributions: 提出一种新颖的神经符号方法，将深度学习与论证构建和推理相结合。
通过可微论证语义实现端到端训练，同时学习特征表示和论证交互。
在表格和图像数据集上达到与标准基线竞争的性能，同时提供可解释的论证推理。
analysis.experiments: 在表格和图像数据集上进行实验，与标准基线比较性能。
limitations: 可能仅适用于分类任务，扩展至其他任务需进一步研究。
论证图的结构约束可能增加训练复杂度。
analysis.primary_area_en: neurosymbolic & hybrid AI systems (physics-informed, logic & formal reasoning, etc.)
analysis.category: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.sub_area: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种神经符号方法Deep Arguing，将深度学习与论证推理结合，实现可解释分类。

papers.title: Agent-First Tool API: A Semantic Interface Paradigm for Enterprise AI Agent Systems
papers.abstract: As AI agents transition from research prototypes to enterprise production systems, the tool interfaces they consume remain rooted in human-oriented CRUD paradigms. This paper identifies five fundamental architectural mismatches between conventional APIs and autonomous agent requirements: exact-identifier dependence, rendering-oriented responses, single-shot interaction assumptions, user-equivalent authorization, and opaque error semantics. We propose the Agent-First Tool API paradigm, comprising three integrated mechanisms: (1) a Six-Verb Semantic Protocol that decomposes tool interactions into search, resolve, preview, execute, verify, and recover phases; (2) a Normalized Tool Contract (NTC) providing structured decision-support metadata including confidence scores, evidence chains, and suggested next actions; and (3) a dual-layer governance pipeline combining static capability policies with dynamic risk escalation. The paradigm is implemented and validated in a production multi-tenant SaaS platform serving 85 registered tools across 6 business domains. Comparative experiments on 50 real operational tasks demonstrate that Agent-First APIs achieve 88% end-to-end task success rate versus 64% for optimized CRUD baselines (+37.5%), while reducing required human interventions by 72.7% and improving autonomous error recovery by 5.8x. We establish that the paradigm is orthogonal and complementary to transport-layer standards such as MCP, operating as the semantic application layer above existing tool discovery and invocation protocols.

analysis.tldr: 提出面向AI Agent的语义化工具API范式，显著提升企业Agent系统任务成功率并减少人工干预。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有工具API基于人类CRUD设计，与自主Agent需求存在根本性架构不匹配，导致Agent在真实企业场景中效率低下、错误频发。
analysis.problem: 解决传统API与AI Agent之间的五个架构不匹配问题：精确标识依赖、渲染导向响应、单次交互假设、用户等价授权和模糊错误语义。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析，但指出传统API在Agent使用中存在系统性失败模式。
analysis.method: 提出Agent-First Tool API范式，包含六动词语义协议、标准化工具契约和双层治理管道三个集成机制。
contributions: 识别并形式化了传统API与Agent之间的五个架构不匹配问题
提出Agent-First Tool API范式，包含六动词语义协议、标准化工具契约和双层治理管道
在生产级多租户SaaS平台上验证，任务成功率提升37.5%，人工干预减少72.7%，错误恢复提升5.8倍
analysis.experiments: 在包含85个注册工具、6个业务领域的生产SaaS平台上，基于50个真实操作任务进行对比实验。
limitations: 实验仅在单一平台进行，泛化性有待验证
未讨论与不同传输层标准（如MCP）的集成细节
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出面向AI Agent的语义化工具API范式，显著提升企业Agent系统任务成功率并减少人工干预。

papers.title: Bridging Sequence and Graph Structure for Epigenetic Age Prediction
papers.abstract: Epigenetic clocks based on DNA methylation have emerged as powerful tools for estimating biological age, with broad applications in aging research, age-related disease studies, and longevity science. Despite advances across machine learning approaches to epigenetic age prediction, spanning penalised linear regression, deep feedforward networks, residual architectures, and graph neural networks, no existing method jointly models co-methylation graph structure and site-specific DNA sequence context within a unified framework. We propose a unified sequence--graph integration framework for epigenetic age prediction that addresses this gap, integrating eight-dimensional DNA sequence statistical features through a lightweight gated modulation mechanism that adaptively scales each site's methylation signal according to its sequence-determined biological relevance prior to graph convolution. Evaluated on 3,707 blood methylation samples against a comprehensive set of baselines, our method achieves a test MAE of 3.149 years, a 12.8\% improvement over the strongest graph-based baseline. Biologically informed statistical features outperform CNN-based sequence encoding, demonstrating that handcrafted sequence features are more effective than end-to-end learned representations in this data regime. Post-hoc interpretability analysis identifies CpG density and local adenine frequency as features with age-dependent importance shifts, consistent with known mechanisms of age-related hypermethylation at CpG-dense promoter regions. Our code is at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: 提出序列-图融合框架用于表观遗传年龄预测，在血液甲基化数据上实现3.149年MAE，比最强图基线提升12.8%。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有表观遗传时钟方法（如线性回归、深度网络、图神经网络）未能联合建模共甲基化图结构和位点特异性DNA序列上下文，限制了预测精度。
analysis.problem: 如何在一个统一框架中同时利用DNA序列特征和共甲基化图结构进行表观遗传年龄预测。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 手工设计的序列统计特征优于CNN端到端学习表示；CpG密度和局部腺嘌呤频率具有年龄依赖性重要性变化。
analysis.method: 提出序列-图融合框架，通过轻量级门控调制机制将八维DNA序列统计特征自适应缩放每个位点的甲基化信号，再输入图卷积网络。
contributions: 首次在统一框架中联合建模共甲基化图结构和DNA序列上下文用于表观遗传年龄预测。
提出轻量级门控调制机制，自适应整合序列特征与甲基化信号。
在3707个血液样本上实现12.8%的MAE改进，并揭示年龄相关甲基化的生物学解释。
analysis.experiments: 在3707个血液甲基化样本上评估，与多种基线比较，测试MAE为3.149年，比最强图基线提升12.8%。
limitations: 仅在血液样本上验证，其他组织泛化性未知。
序列特征为手工设计，可能未捕获全部序列信息。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出序列-图融合框架用于表观遗传年龄预测，在血液甲基化数据上实现3.149年MAE，比最强图基线提升12.8%。

papers.title: A Reflective Storytelling Agent for Older Adults: Integrating Argumentation Schemes and Argument Mining in LLM-Based Personalised Narratives
papers.abstract: This work investigates whether knowledge-driven large language model (LLM)-based storytelling can support purposeful narrative interaction with a digital companion for older adults. To address known limitations of LLMs, including hallucinations and limited transparency, we present a reflective storytelling agent integrating knowledge graphs, user modelling, argumentation theory, and argument mining to guide and inspect narrative generation. The study consisted of two phases. Phase I employed participatory design involving 11 domain experts in a formative evaluation that informed iterative refinement. The resulting system generates narratives grounded in structured user models representing health-promoting activities and motivations. Phase II involved 55 older adults evaluating persona-based narratives across four prompts and two creativity levels. Participants assessed perceived purpose, usefulness, cultural relatability, and inconsistencies. The system additionally computed hallucination-risk indicators to evaluate generated narratives. Participants recognised personally relevant purposes in roughly two thirds of narratives, while argument-based purposes were identified in around half of these cases. Cultural recognisability strongly influenced willingness to use the functionality, whereas minor inconsistencies were often tolerated when narratives remained understandable and personally relevant. Narratives with higher hallucination-risk indicators were more often perceived as inconsistent, while higher argument-quality indicators tended to co-occur with higher clarity and meaningfulness ratings. Overall, the study positions argument mining as a reflective inspection mechanism for comparing formal grounding signals with human evaluations in health-oriented LLM storytelling for older adults.

analysis.tldr: 结合知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘，构建面向老年人的反思性叙事Agent，提升LLM故事生成的可信度与个性化。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM在叙事生成中存在幻觉和透明度不足的问题，而面向老年人的健康促进叙事需要高可信度和个性化，现有方法难以满足。
analysis.problem: 如何利用论证理论和论证挖掘来引导和检查LLM生成的个性化叙事，使其对老年人更具目的性、可信度和文化相关性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出：参与者约三分之二的叙事中识别出个人相关目的，约一半案例中识别出基于论证的目的；文化可识别性强烈影响使用意愿，而轻微不一致在叙事可理解且相关时被容忍；高幻觉风险指标与高不一致感知相关，高论证质量指标与高清晰度和有意义性评分相关。
analysis.method: 提出反思性叙事Agent，集成知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘，以引导和检查LLM生成的个性化叙事。
contributions: 提出一种结合论证方案和论证挖掘的LLM叙事生成框架，用于面向老年人的健康促进叙事。
通过两阶段研究（专家参与式设计和55名老年人评估）验证了方法的有效性，并分析了幻觉风险、论证质量与用户感知的关系。
analysis.experiments: 两阶段研究：第一阶段11名领域专家参与式设计；第二阶段55名老年人评估基于人设的叙事，涉及4个提示和2个创造力水平，评估感知目的、有用性、文化相关性和不一致性，并计算幻觉风险指标。
limitations: 样本量有限（55名老年人），可能影响泛化性。
论证挖掘的质量依赖于预定义方案，可能无法覆盖所有叙事类型。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 结合知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘，构建面向老年人的反思性叙事Agent，提升LLM故事生成的可信度与个性化。

papers.title: PrimeKG-CL: A Continual Graph Learning Benchmark on Evolving Biomedical Knowledge Graphs
papers.abstract: Biomedical knowledge graphs underwrite drug repurposing and clinical decision support, yet the upstream ontologies they depend on update on independent cycles that add millions of edges and deprecate hundreds of thousands more between releases. Yet existing continual graph learning has been studied almost exclusively on synthetic random splits of static, generic KGs, a regime that cannot reproduce the asynchronous, structured evolution real biomedical KGs undergo. To this end, we introduce PrimeKG-CL, a CGL benchmark built from nine authoritative biomedical databases (129K+ nodes, 8.1M+ edges, 10 node types, 30 relation types) with two genuine temporal snapshots (June 2021, July 2023; 5.83M edges added, 889K removed, 7.21M persistent), 10 entity-type-grouped tasks, multimodal node features, and a per-task persistent/added/removed test stratification. On three tasks (biomedical relationship prediction, entity classification, KGQA), we evaluate six CL strategies across four KGE decoders, plus LKGE, an LLM-RAG agent, and CMKL. We find that decoder choice and continual learning strategy interact strongly: no single strategy performs best across all decoders, and mismatched combinations can significantly degrade performance. Moreover, only DistMult exhibits a clear separation between persistent and deprecated knowledge, indicating that standard metrics conflate retention of still-valid facts with failure to forget outdated ones; this effect is absent under RotatE. In addition, multimodal features improve entity-level tasks by up to 60%, and a recent CKGE framework (IncDE) failed to scale to our 5.67M-triple base task across five attempts up to 350GB RAM. Data, pipeline, baselines, and the stratified split are released openly. Dataset: this http URL |Code: this http URL

analysis.tldr: 首个基于真实生物医学知识图谱演化的持续图学习基准，揭示解码器与持续学习策略的强交互及标准评测指标的缺陷。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有持续图学习研究几乎完全基于静态通用知识图谱的合成随机分割，无法模拟真实生物医学知识图谱的异步、结构化演化过程，导致方法评估脱离实际应用场景。
analysis.problem: 缺乏一个能反映真实生物医学知识图谱动态演化（节点/边新增、废弃）的持续图学习基准，以系统评估不同方法在关系预测、实体分类和知识图谱问答等任务上的表现。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 解码器选择与持续学习策略存在强交互，无单一策略在所有解码器上最优，错误组合会显著降低性能；DistMult解码器能清晰区分持久知识与废弃知识，而RotatE则不能，说明标准指标混淆了保留有效事实与遗忘过时事实的能力；多模态特征可提升实体级任务性能达60%；现有CKGE框架（IncDE）无法扩展到大规模基任务。
analysis.method: 构建PrimeKG-CL基准：整合9个权威生物医学数据库，提供两个真实时间快照（2021年6月、2023年7月），设计10个实体类型分组任务，包含多模态节点特征和分层测试集（持久/新增/移除），并评估6种持续学习策略、4种KGE解码器、LKGE（LLM-RAG智能体）和CMKL。
contributions: 首个基于真实生物医学知识图谱演化的持续图学习基准，包含大规模节点、边和多种任务类型。
揭示解码器与持续学习策略的强交互效应，以及标准指标在区分持久与废弃知识上的缺陷。
公开数据集、代码、基线和分层分割，促进可重复研究。
analysis.experiments: 在三个任务（生物医学关系预测、实体分类、知识图谱问答）上评估了六种持续学习策略、四种KGE解码器、LKGE和CMKL，并分析了多模态特征的影响及IncDE的可扩展性。
limitations: 仅基于两个时间快照，可能无法完全捕捉更细粒度的演化模式。
未探索更复杂的持续学习策略或解码器组合。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 首个基于真实生物医学知识图谱演化的持续图学习基准，揭示解码器与持续学习策略的强交互及标准评测指标的缺陷。

papers.title: Consistency as a Testable Property: Statistical Methods to Evaluate AI Agent Reliability
papers.abstract: This paper establishes a rigorous measurement science for AI agent reliability, providing a foundational framework for quantifying consistency under semantically preserving perturbations. By leveraging $U$-statistics for output-level reliability and kernel-based metrics for trajectory-level stability, we offer a principled approach to evaluating agents across diverse operating conditions. Our proposal highlights the important distinction between the core capability and execution robustness of an agent, showing that minor task-level variations can induce complete strategy breakdowns despite the agent possessing the requisite knowledge for the task. We validate our framework through extensive experiments on three agentic benchmarks, demonstrating that trajectory-level consistency metrics provide far greater diagnostic sensitivity than traditional pass@1 rates. By providing the mathematical tools to isolate where and why agents deviate, we enable the identification and rectification of architectural concerns that hinder the deployment of agents in high-stakes, real-world environments.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于U统计量和核方法的AI智能体可靠性评估框架，通过轨迹级一致性度量诊断策略崩溃。
analysis.research_motivation: AI智能体在高风险场景部署时缺乏可靠的评估方法，传统pass@1指标无法检测语义保持扰动下的策略崩溃。
analysis.problem: 如何量化AI智能体在语义保持扰动下的输出一致性和轨迹稳定性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，微小任务变化可导致智能体策略完全崩溃，即使其具备完成任务所需的知识。
analysis.method: 利用U统计量评估输出级可靠性，基于核方法度量轨迹级稳定性，区分核心能力与执行鲁棒性。
contributions: 建立了AI智能体可靠性的严格度量科学框架
提出轨迹级一致性指标，比pass@1具有更高诊断灵敏度
提供数学工具定位智能体偏差原因
analysis.experiments: 在三个智能体基准上验证，轨迹级一致性指标比传统pass@1更敏感。
limitations: 未提及跨领域泛化性
可能依赖特定扰动类型
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于U统计量和核方法的AI智能体可靠性评估框架，通过轨迹级一致性度量诊断策略崩溃。

papers.title: SLASH the Sink: Sharpening Structural Attention Inside LLMs
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show remarkable semantic understanding but often struggle with structural understanding when processing graph topologies in a serialized format. Existing solutions rely on training external graph-based adapters or fine-tuning, which incur high costs and lost generalizability. In this work, we investigate the internal mechanisms of LLMs and present a critical finding: LLMs spontaneously reconstruct the graph's topology internally, evidenced by a distinct "sawtooth" pattern in their attention maps that structurally aligns with the "token-level adjacency matrix". However, this intrinsic structural understanding is diluted by the attention sink. We theoretically formalize this dilution as a representation bottleneck, stemming from a fundamental conflict: the model's anisotropic bias, essential for language tasks, suppresses the topology-aware local aggregation required for graph reasoning. To address this, we propose a training-free solution, named StructuraL Attention SHarpening (Slash), which amplifies this internal structural understanding via a plug-and-play attention redistribution. Experiments on pure graph tasks and molecular prediction validate Slash delivers significant and consistent performance gains across diverse LLMs.

analysis.tldr: 无需训练，通过锐化注意力分布增强LLM对图结构的内部理解。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM在序列化处理图拓扑时结构理解能力不足，现有方法依赖外部图适配器或微调，成本高且泛化性差。
analysis.problem: LLM内部对图结构的理解被注意力汇聚（attention sink）稀释，导致结构推理能力受限。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: LLM注意力图呈现与token级邻接矩阵结构对齐的锯齿模式，表明其内部自发重建图拓扑，但该理解被注意力汇聚稀释。
analysis.method: 提出Slash方法，通过即插即用的注意力重分布锐化结构注意力，无需训练。
contributions: 发现LLM内部自发重建图拓扑的证据（注意力图中的锯齿模式）。
理论形式化注意力汇聚导致的结构理解稀释为表示瓶颈。
提出无需训练的注意力锐化方法Slash，提升LLM在图任务上的性能。
analysis.experiments: 在纯图任务和分子预测上验证，Slash在不同LLM上均取得显著且一致的性能提升。
limitations: 方法依赖LLM内部已有的结构理解，对完全不理解图结构的模型可能无效。
仅针对序列化图输入，未考虑其他结构化数据形式。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 无需训练，通过锐化注意力分布增强LLM对图结构的内部理解。

papers.title: SkillEvolver: Skill Learning as a Meta-Skill
papers.abstract: Agent skills today are static artifact: authored once -- by human curation or one-shot generation from parametric knowledge -- and then consumed unchanged, with no mechanism to improve from real use. We propose \textbf{SkillEvolver}, a lightweight, plug-and-play solution for online skill learning, in which a single meta-skill iteratively authors, deploys, and refines domain-specific skills. The learning target of SkillEvolver is the skill's prose and code, not model weights, so that the resulting artifact drops into any agent without retraining; and the meta-skill itself is just another skill, loaded through the same interface by any protocol-compliant CLI-agent. Unlike trace-distillation, the meta-skill refines only after deploying the learnt skill, such that the learning signal comes from failures another agent encounters while using it -- not from exploratory traces alone. Refinement iterations are governed by a fresh-agent overfit audit that catches possible leakage as well as deployed-skill-specific failures, including the silent-bypass mode in which a skill appears valid in content but is never invoked at runtime. On $83$ SkillsBench tasks spanning $15^{+}$ domains, SkillEvolver reaches $56.8\%$ accuracy versus $43.6\%$ for curated human skills and $29.9\%$ for the no-skill baseline; on three GPU kernel optimization tasks from KernelBench, it also raises mean speedup from $1.16$ to $1.51$ on average.

analysis.tldr: SkillEvolver提出一种元技能，通过迭代编写、部署和精炼领域技能，使智能体技能能从实际使用中持续改进。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前智能体技能是静态的，一旦创建便无法从实际使用中改进，限制了其适应性和性能。
analysis.problem: 如何让智能体技能在部署后能自动从使用反馈中学习并提升，而无需重新训练模型。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出静态技能存在性能瓶颈，而基于轨迹蒸馏的方法缺乏真实部署反馈；同时存在“静默绕过”模式，即技能内容看似有效但运行时从未被调用。
analysis.method: SkillEvolver是一个轻量级元技能，它通过迭代方式编写、部署和精炼领域技能，学习目标为技能的文本描述和代码，而非模型权重；精炼过程基于其他智能体使用该技能时遇到的失败，并通过新智能体过拟合审计来检测泄漏和特定失败模式。
contributions: 提出一种在线技能学习框架，使技能能从实际使用中持续改进。
设计新智能体过拟合审计机制，检测技能泄漏和静默绕过等失败模式。
在83个SkillsBench任务和3个KernelBench任务上验证了有效性，显著超越人工技能和无技能基线。
analysis.experiments: 在83个SkillsBench任务（涵盖15+领域）上，SkillEvolver达到56.8%准确率，优于人工技能的43.6%和无技能基线的29.9%；在3个GPU内核优化任务上，平均加速比从1.16提升至1.51。
limitations: 可能依赖初始技能的质量和领域覆盖范围。
审计机制可能增加计算开销。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: SkillEvolver提出一种元技能，通过迭代编写、部署和精炼领域技能，使智能体技能能从实际使用中持续改进。

papers.title: ASIA: an Autonomous System Identification Agent
papers.abstract: Over the years, research in system identification has provided a rich set of methods for learning dynamical models, together with well-established theoretical guarantees. In practice, however, the choice of model class, training algorithm, and hyperparameter tuning is still largely left to empirical trial-and-error, requiring substantial expert time and domain experience. Motivated by recent advances in agentic artificial intelligence, we present ASIA, a framework that delegates this iterative search to a large language model acting as an autonomous coding agent. Building on existing agentic platforms, ASIA closes the loop between hypothesis, implementation, and evaluation without human intervention, requiring only a plain-English description of the identification problem. We conduct an empirical study of ASIA on two system identification benchmarks and analyse the agent's search behaviour, the architectures and training strategies it discovers, and the quality of the resulting models. We also discuss the potential of the approach and its current limitations, including implicit test leakage, reduced methodological transparency, and reproducibility concerns.

analysis.tldr: ASIA 利用 LLM 作为自主编码代理，自动完成系统辨识中的模型选择、训练和调参，无需人工干预。
analysis.research_motivation: 系统辨识方法丰富但实际应用依赖人工试错，耗费专家时间和经验，亟需自动化。
analysis.problem: 如何自动化系统辨识中的模型类选择、训练算法和超参数调优，减少人工干预。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 基于 LLM 的自主编码代理框架，闭环执行假设、实现和评估，仅需自然语言描述问题。
contributions: 提出 ASIA 框架，将系统辨识的迭代搜索委托给 LLM 代理。
在两个基准上实证分析代理的搜索行为、发现的架构和训练策略。
analysis.experiments: 在两个系统辨识基准上进行实证研究，分析搜索行为、架构和训练策略。
limitations: 存在隐式测试泄露风险。
方法透明度降低，可重复性存疑。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: ASIA 利用 LLM 作为自主编码代理，自动完成系统辨识中的模型选择、训练和调参，无需人工干预。

papers.title: Can Agent Benchmarks Support Their Scores? Evidence-Supported Bounds for Interactive-Agent Evaluation
papers.abstract: Interactive agent benchmarks map an agent run to a binary outcome through outcome checks. When these checks rely on surface level signals or fail to capture the agent's actual action path, they cannot reliably determine whether the run succeeded. For example, a benchmark task may ask whether Alice's shipping address was changed, while the outcome check only verifies that the agent clicked "Save." This does not guarantee that the intended state change occurred, since the agent may have modified the wrong record. Treating such a run as successful therefore makes the reported score misleading. Benchmark quality thus depends not only on task design, but also on the reliability of outcome detection. We address this problem by introducing an outcome evidence reporting layer for existing benchmarks, without modifying their tasks, agents, or evaluators. The layer performs three functions. First, before scoring, it specifies which stored artifacts are required to verify the claimed outcome for each case. Second, it applies a locked checklist to each completed run and assigns one of three evidence labels: Evidence Pass, Evidence Fail, or Unknown. Third, it reports evidence supported score bounds that quantify uncertainty arising from Unknown cases. Rather than silently counting, discarding, or hiding uncertain cases inside a single aggregate success rate, the framework keeps them explicitly visible. We evaluate the outcome evidence layer on five public benchmarks: ANDROIDWORLD, AGENTDOJO, APPWORLD, tau3 bench retail, and MINIWOB. The resulting reports separate several empirically distinct failure modes.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种证据支持层，为交互式Agent基准测试提供更可靠的分数边界，揭示表面检查导致的评分偏差。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有交互式Agent基准测试依赖表面信号进行结果检查，无法可靠判断任务是否真正成功，导致报告分数具有误导性。
analysis.problem: 基准测试的结果检测不可靠，表面检查无法保证实际状态变化，需要量化不确定性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，基准测试中结果检查仅验证点击等表面动作，而非实际状态变化，导致虚假成功案例。
analysis.method: 引入结果证据报告层，包含三项功能：1) 指定验证所需存储工件；2) 应用锁定检查表分配证据标签（证据通过、证据失败、未知）；3) 报告证据支持的分数边界，量化未知案例的不确定性。
contributions: 提出证据支持层，无需修改任务、Agent或评估器即可提升基准可靠性。
在五个公开基准上评估，分离出多种经验性失败模式。
analysis.experiments: 在ANDROIDWORLD、AGENTDOJO、APPWORLD、tau3 bench retail和MINIWOB五个基准上评估，摘要未提供具体数值结果。
limitations: 可能依赖人工定义工件和检查表，扩展性受限。
未知案例的处理可能增加评估复杂度。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种证据支持层，为交互式Agent基准测试提供更可靠的分数边界，揭示表面检查导致的评分偏差。

papers.title: LLM4Branch: Large Language Model for Discovering Efficient Branching Policies of Integer Programs
papers.abstract: Efficient branching policies are essential for accelerating Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) solvers. Their design has long relied on hand-crafted heuristics, and now machine learning has emerged as a promising paradigm to automate this process. However, existing learning-based methods are often hindered by their dependence on expensive expert demonstrations and the gap between training objectives and the solver's end-to-end performance. In this work, we propose LLM4Branch, a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the discovery of efficient branching policies. Specifically, the discovered policy is an executable program with a program skeleton generated by the LLM and a parameter vector, which is optimized via a zeroth-order method over a few instances with their end-to-end performance feedback. Extensive experiments on standard MILP benchmarks demonstrate that LLM4Branch establishes a new state-of-the-art among CPU-based methods and achieves performance competitive with advanced GPU-based models. Codes are available at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: 利用LLM自动发现高效MILP分支策略，通过程序骨架和零阶优化实现SOTA。
analysis.research_motivation: MILP求解器的分支策略长期依赖手工启发式，现有学习方法依赖昂贵专家演示且训练目标与求解器端到端性能存在差距。
analysis.problem: 自动发现高效的MILP分支策略，以加速求解器性能。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: LLM生成可执行程序骨架，结合参数向量，通过零阶优化在少量实例上基于端到端性能反馈优化参数。
contributions: 提出LLM4Branch框架，利用LLM自动发现分支策略。
在标准MILP基准上达到CPU方法SOTA，性能与先进GPU方法竞争。
analysis.experiments: 在标准MILP基准上进行了广泛实验，显示LLM4Branch在CPU方法中达到SOTA，并与GPU方法性能相当。
limitations: 可能依赖LLM生成程序骨架的质量。
零阶优化在复杂问题上可能效率较低。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 利用LLM自动发现高效MILP分支策略，通过程序骨架和零阶优化实现SOTA。

papers.title: GuardAD: Safeguarding Autonomous Driving MLLMs via Markovian Safety Logic
papers.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly integrated into autonomous driving (AD) systems; however, they remain vulnerable to diverse safety threats, particularly in accident-prone scenarios. Recent safeguard mechanisms have shown promise by incorporating logical constraints, yet most rely on static formulations that lack temporally grounded safety reasoning over evolving traffic interactions, resulting in limited robustness in dynamic driving environments. To address these limitations, we propose GuardAD, a model-agnostic safeguard that formulates AD safety as an evolving Markovian logical state. GuardAD introduces Neuro-Symbolic Logic Formalization, which represents safety predicates over heterogeneous traffic participants and continuously induces them via n-th order Markovian Logic Induction. This design enables the inference of emerging and latent hazards beyond single-step observations. Rather than simply vetoing unsafe actions, GuardAD performs Logic-Driven Action Revision, where inferred safety states actively guide action refinement without modifying the underlying MLLM. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks and AD-MLLMs demonstrate that GuardAD substantially reduces accident rates (-32.07%) while slightly improving task performance (+6.85%). Moreover, closed-loop simulation evaluations, together with physical-world vehicle studies, further validate the effectiveness and potential of GuardAD.

analysis.tldr: GuardAD 通过马尔可夫逻辑状态推理，为自动驾驶多模态大模型提供动态安全防护，显著降低事故率。
analysis.research_motivation: 自动驾驶 MLLM 面临多种安全威胁，现有防护机制缺乏对动态交通交互的时序安全推理，鲁棒性不足。
analysis.problem: 如何为自动驾驶 MLLM 设计一种能随时间演化、推理潜在危险的安全防护机制。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 现有安全防护依赖静态逻辑约束，无法处理动态驾驶环境中不断变化的交通交互，导致鲁棒性有限。
analysis.method: 提出 GuardAD，包含神经符号逻辑形式化（将安全谓词表示为马尔可夫逻辑状态）和逻辑驱动动作修正（根据推理出的安全状态引导动作优化）。
contributions: 提出一种模型无关的防护框架，将自动驾驶安全建模为演化的马尔可夫逻辑状态。
引入 n 阶马尔可夫逻辑归纳，实现超越单步观测的潜在危险推理。
通过逻辑驱动动作修正，在不修改底层 MLLM 的情况下提升安全性。
analysis.experiments: 在多个基准和 AD-MLLM 上，事故率降低 32.07%，任务性能提升 6.85%；闭环仿真和实车实验验证了有效性。
limitations: 可能依赖高质量的安全谓词定义。
马尔可夫阶数选择可能影响推理效果。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: GuardAD 通过马尔可夫逻辑状态推理，为自动驾驶多模态大模型提供动态安全防护，显著降低事故率。

papers.title: Agentic Performance at the Edge: Insights from Benchmarking
papers.abstract: Agentic artificial intelligence (AI) is a natural fit for Internet of Things (IoT) and edge systems, but edge deployments are often constrained to models around 8 billion parameters or smaller. An important question is: How much agentic-task quality is lost when model size is constrained by memory, power, and latency budgets? To address this question, in this paper, we provide an initial empirical study considering edge-focused model scaling, general-purpose versus coder-oriented model effects, and tool-enabled execution under a fixed protocol. We introduce a domain-conditioned evaluation methodology, an implementation-grounded analysis of model-tool interactions, practical guidance for model selection under constraints, and an analysis of failure modes that reveals distinct semantic versus execution failure patterns across model families. Our core finding is that edge-agent quality is not a simple function of parameter count. Robust deployment depends on the joint design of model choice and tool workflow. Domain-conditioned analysis reveals Pareto fronts in the accuracy-latency space that can guide strategy selection based on operational priorities.

analysis.tldr: 边缘设备上的智能体性能并非仅由参数量决定，模型选择与工具流程的联合设计至关重要。
analysis.research_motivation: 物联网和边缘系统受限于内存、功耗和延迟，通常只能部署约8B参数以下的模型，但缺乏对模型规模压缩后智能体任务质量损失的量化研究。
analysis.problem: 在边缘部署约束下，如何评估和选择模型以最大化智能体任务性能？
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出领域条件评估方法，通过固定协议下的工具执行实验，分析边缘模型缩放、通用型与编码型模型差异及模型-工具交互。
contributions: 提出领域条件评估方法用于边缘智能体基准测试
提供模型-工具交互的实现级分析
给出约束下模型选择的实用指南
揭示不同模型家族中语义失败与执行失败的差异模式
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 仅基于初始实证研究，可能缺乏大规模验证
未覆盖所有边缘硬件和模型类型
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 边缘设备上的智能体性能并非仅由参数量决定，模型选择与工具流程的联合设计至关重要。

papers.title: Agent-X: Full Pipeline Acceleration of On-device AI Agents
papers.abstract: LLM-based agents deliver state-of-the-art performance across tasks but incur high end-to-end latency on edge devices. We introduce Agent-X, a software-only, accuracy-preserving framework that accelerates both the prefill and decode stages of on-device agent workloads. Agent-X's two key components rewrite prompts to leverage prefix caching tailored to agent-specific input-token patterns and enable LLM-free speculative decoding for fast token generation with minimal overhead. On representative agentic workloads, Agent-X achieves a 1.61x end-to-end speedup in real systems with no accuracy loss and can be seamlessly integrated into existing on-device AI agents. To the best of our knowledge, ours is the first to systematically characterize and eliminate latency bottlenecks in on-device agents.

analysis.tldr: Agent-X 通过提示重写和免 LLM 推测解码，实现端到端 1.61 倍加速且无精度损失。
analysis.research_motivation: 基于 LLM 的智能体在边缘设备上端到端延迟高，影响实际部署体验。
analysis.problem: 如何在不损失精度的前提下，加速边缘设备上 LLM 智能体的预填充和解码阶段。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出 Agent-X 框架，包含两个关键组件：1) 重写提示以利用前缀缓存，适配智能体特有的输入令牌模式；2) 免 LLM 推测解码，实现快速令牌生成。
contributions: 首个系统性地刻画并消除边缘设备上智能体延迟瓶颈的工作。
提出软件仅、保精度的加速框架，实现 1.61 倍端到端加速。
analysis.experiments: 在代表性智能体工作负载上，Agent-X 实现 1.61 倍端到端加速且无精度损失。
limitations: 可能仅适用于特定类型的智能体工作负载。
免 LLM 推测解码的通用性有待验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: Agent-X 通过提示重写和免 LLM 推测解码，实现端到端 1.61 倍加速且无精度损失。

papers.title: Autonomous FAIR Digital Objects: From Passive Assertions to Active Knowledge
papers.abstract: Scientific knowledge on the Web is published as passive assertions and cannot decide when to validate evidence, reconcile contradictions, or update confidence as findings accumulate.
